Block.Para: First Session, Thirty-ninth Parliament,
55-56 Elizabeth II, 2006-2007
HOUSE OF COMMONS OF CANADA
BILL C-417
An Act to establish a Memorial Wall for Canada's fallen soldiers and peacekeepers
first reading, March 27, 2007

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca
SUMMARY

This enactment requires the Minister of National Defence to establish a Memorial Wall that will comprise the names of Canadaâs fallen soldiers and peacekeepers and have it located on a suitable area of public land.

Bill_Part.RunningHead_E: Canadian Soldiers' and Peacekeepers' Memorial Wall

Block.Para: 1st Session, 39th Parliament,
55-56 Elizabeth II, 2006-2007
house of commons of canada
BILL C-417
An Act to establish a Memorial Wall for Canada's fallen soldiers and peacekeepers
WHEREAS Canada has yet to properly honour, in a suitable location that is accessible to the public at all times, all of our fallen soldiers and peacekeepers;
WHEREAS over 115,000 of our fallen soldiers and peacekeepers have their graves in seventy-five countries and hundreds of cemeteries around the world;
WHEREAS their remains cannot be repatriated to Canada;
WHEREAS we must establish a suitable national shrine to honour our fallen soldiers and peacekeepers;
AND WHEREAS proper recognition for our fallen soldiers and peacekeepers will show our love for them and our respect for their sacrifice;
NOW, THEREFORE, Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. This Act may be cited as the Canadian Soldiersâ and Peacekeepersâ Memorial Wall Act.
2. The following definitions apply in this Act.

Block.MarginalNote:
âMemorial Wallâ
Â« mur commÃ©moratif Â»
Block.Para: âMemorial Wallâ means the Memorial Wall established under section 3.
âMinisterâ means the Minister of National Defence.

Block.MarginalNote:
âpublic landâ
Â« terres publiques Â»
Block.Para: âpublic landâ means an area of land owned by the Crown that is accessible to the public at all times.
3. (1) The Minister shall establish a Memo-rial Wall comprising the names of all of Canadaâs fallen soldiers and peacekeepers.

Block.MarginalNote: Design and location of Memorial Wall
Block.Para: (2) The Minister shall determine the design of the Memorial Wall and a suitable area of public land for the memorial wall to be located.

4. The Minister shall collect the names of all Canadian soldiers and peacekeepers who die or have died during international peacekeeping missions and wars, and record their names on the Memorial Wall.
5. The Memorial Wall shall be completed not later than two years after this Act comes into force.

Block.MarginalNote: Memorial Wall to be updated annually
Block.Para: 6. The list of names on the Memorial Wall shall be brought up to date no less than once a year.

Published under authority of the Speaker of the House of Commons
